description: Finalized early 2025. Includes simplifying, straightening, and consolidating routes, $30 million of new/increased service, new 'rush' limited routes. Set to go into effect June 2025. Did not include new SBS routes.
Reimagined bus network for Queens, driven by customer feedback, implementing Rush routes and modernized service across the borough

The Queens Bus Network Redesign aims to create a more efficient, reliable, and accessible bus system that better serves the needs of all riders, while prioritizing equity to address disparities in access and opportunity.

Proposed Final Plan for redesigning the Queens bus network, published Q4 2023. Proposes 121 total routes (91 local, 30 express) vs 113 existing routes. Includes bus priority expansion with NYC DOT, overnight network expansion, all-day frequent network expansion, and bus stop spacing changes.
Queens Bus Network Redesign Proposed Final Plan Addendum published December 2024
Board staff summary seeking approval to implement the Queens Bus Network Redesign as set forth in the December 2024 Proposed Final Plan Addendum for local and express bus service in Queens.

MTA's borough-wide plan to improve bus service in Queens, launched in 2019. Coordinating with NYC DOT on targeted corridors, including Grand Av/Grand St. Published Proposed Final Plan in December 2023.
Queens Bus Network Redesign New Draft Plan released in March 2022, one of the largest bus network redesigns in the country with over 100 routes
Targeting to finalize Queens Bus Network Redesign next year
Seeks to modernize the borough's bus network through a comprehensive redesign to deliver faster, more frequent, and more reliable service. Proposed Final Plan announced in December 2023.

Proposal to redesign the Queens bus network to better match ridership demands while improving service for customers. Simpler, more direct routes, improved bus stop balancing, enhanced connectivity, improved frequency on key routes, and expanded bus priority.
Borough-by-borough bus network redesign process; Proposed Final Plan for more robust bus service released in December 2023; public consultation process in March 2024
The Committee authorized a public hearing on the Queens Bus Network Redesign
Queens Bus network redesign focused on simplifying and improving service for customers with more reliable service, faster travel, better connections, and improved ease of use
Queens Bus network redesign focused on simplifying and improving service for customers with more reliable service, faster travel, better connections, and improved ease of use.

Support for the Queens Bus Network Redesign, including funding for an additional 47 bus operators and 10 managers at NYCT and an additional 78 positions at MTA Bus Company (NYC Funded). Funded as a New Need/Investment in the November Financial Plan.
Queens network redesign mentioned as a priority initiative for expense growth
Queens Bus Network Redesign New Needs/Investments: $13 million in 2025, $25 million in 2026, $26 million in 2027 and 2028
Proposed redesign of Queens bus network aiming to improve route design, connectivity, frequency, and accessibility. Will be implemented in two phases, scheduled for summer and fall of 2025.
Public hearing held in Queens to discuss the Queens Bus Network Redesign; feedback provided by customers will help inform next steps.
Increase Appeal for Customers - Provide 21st Century Bus Service

Board approval to implement the Queens Bus Network Redesign described in the December 2024 Proposed Final Plan Addendum for local and express bus service in Queens. Includes 49 existing routes with routing changes, 17 new routes, and four retired routes.
The Board approved the Queens Bus Network Redesign on February 26, 2025. Chair Lieber stated it would be funded by savings identified through the efficiency effort.
Redesign of Queens bus network, rolling out in two phases at end of June and end of August
Bus network redesign for Queens with two-phase implementation

Transformative initiative launching in Phase 1 on June 29, 2025. 16 new routes will launch, 67 routes will be modified, 5 discontinued in Phase One. In Phase Two, 1 new route, 37 modified, 1 discontinued.
Historic effort to bring new and improved transit service to Queens, introducing rush routes and enhanced coverage. Phase 1 takes effect June 29, 2025.

Historic effort to bring new and improved transit service to Queens. Includes introduction of rush routes, enhanced coverage in Queens neighborhoods, and a promotional free transfer policy allowing Queens bus riders tapping with OMNY one additional free bus transfer within a three-hour period.
Launched Phase 1 in Eastern Queens; Phase 2 in Northwest and Southwest Queens starting August 31
Temporary impact of hiring for Queens Bus Network Redesign in advance of (QBNR) beginning
Queens Bus Network Redesign including route changes for Q102, B62, Q103 starting August 21

One of the largest and most complex bus network redesigns in North American history, implemented in two phases during summer 2025. Introduced a modernized bus network for Queens. Six-year effort with three rounds of proposals, more than 18,000 comments, over 250 events.
Full rollout of a new bus network for Queens, launched in two phases over summer 2025, added 11 new routes, expanded overnight service, improved transit access for 68,000 more residents

payload.description: Piloting security monitor screens on over 100 buses this year; high-definition LCD customer-facing screen streaming live video feeds from existing security camera infrastructure, deployed to routes with highest rates of assaults
Cameras being deployed in every conductor cab; part of efforts improving frontline worker safety

description: First of four pilot stations receiving platform barriers at 191 St on the 1 line

payload.description: Replacement for malformed operated_by entity -> entity relation; source jointly names New York City Transit and MTA Bus as operators of subway and bus service in New York City, but does not support one entity as operated_by the other.

records.display_name: Detectable Warning Strips on subway platforms

payload.description: Multi-year project to install yellow detectable warning strips (DWS) on the edges of more than 50 subway platforms over the past two years
Bollards installed around the conductor cab on both platforms at 125-Lexington Avenue 4 5 6 as part of the No Standing Pilot
